Name: Surskit
Flavor-Text: It secretes a thick, sweet-scented syrup from the tip of its head. It lives on weed-choked ponds.

Name: Masquerain
Effects: Discard a random Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The antennae have distinctive patterns that look like eyes. When it rains, they grow heavy, making flight impossible.

Name: Brute Bonnet
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It bears a slight resemblance to a Pokémon described in a dubious magazine as a cross between a dinosaur and a mushroom.

Name: Slither Wing
Effects: This Pokémon also does 60 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon somewhat resembles an ancient form of Volcarona that was introduced in a dubious magazine.

Name: Iron Moth
Effects: Flip 3 coins. If 1 of them is heads, this attack does 20 more damage. If 2 of them are heads, this attack does 50 more damage. If all of them are heads, this attack does 120 more damage.
Flavor-Text: No records exist of this species being caught. Data is lacking, but the Pokémon’s traits match up with an object described in an old book.

Name: Psyduck
Flavor-Text: If its chronic headache peaks, it may exhibit odd powers. It seems unable to recall such an episode.

Name: Golduck
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It swims gracefully along on the quiet, slow- moving rivers and lakes of which it is so fond.

Name: Vaporeon
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.
Flavor-Text: Its cell composition is similar to water molecules. As a result, it can’t be seen when it melts away into water.

Name: Buizel
Flavor-Text: It inflates its flotation sac, keeping its face above water in order to watch for prey movement.

Name: Floatzel
Flavor-Text: It is a common sight around fishing ports. It is known to rescue people and help fishers carry what they caught.

Name: Snom
Flavor-Text: Within its internal organs, Snom amplifies the frigid air it gets from eating snow and then uses this amplified air to create icicle-like spikes.

Name: Frosmoth
Flavor-Text: It causes blizzards as it flies around with its huge, chill-emanating wings. Clean meltwater is its favorite thing to drink.

Name: Iron Bundle ex
Effects: If this is the first time this Pokémon has used an attack after coming into play, this attack does 20 more damage, and your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

Name: Pawmi
Flavor-Text: The pads of its paws are electricity-discharging organs. Pawmi fires electricity from its forepaws while standing unsteadily on its hind legs.

Name: Pawmo
Effects: This attack also does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Pawmo uses a unique fighting technique in which it uses its forepaws to strike foes and zap them with electricity from its paw pads simultaneously.

Name: Pawmot
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Pawmot’s fluffy fur acts as a battery. It can store the same amount of electricity as an electric car.

Name: Iron Hands
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 70 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon shares many similarities with Iron Hands, an object mentioned in a certain expedition journal.

Name: Iron Thorns
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Some of its notable features match those of an object named within a certain expedition journal as Iron Thorns.

Name: Miraidon ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you put this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench, you may switch it with your Active Pokémon. If you do, move all of your Energy in play to this Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each [L] Energy attached to this Pokémon.

Name: Espeon
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It uses the fine hair that covers its body to sense air currents and predict its enemy’s actions.

Name: Flittle
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It spends its time running around wastelands. If anyone steals its beloved berries, it will chase them down and exact its revenge.

Name: Espathra
Effects: During your next turn, the Defending Pokémon takes +50 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text: It emits psychic power from the gaps between its multicolored frills and sprints at speeds greater than 120 mph.

Name: Greavard
Flavor-Text: This friendly Pokémon doesn’t like being alone. Pay it even the slightest bit of attention, and it will follow you forever.

Name: Houndstone
Flavor-Text: A lovingly mourned Pokémon was reborn as Houndstone. It doesn’t like anyone touching the protuberance atop its head.

Name: Scream Tail
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, discard your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It resembles a mysterious Pokémon described in a paranormal magazine as a Jigglypuff from one billion years ago.

Name: Flutter Mane ex
Effects: If this is the first time this Pokémon has used an attack after coming into play, during your opponent's next turn, they can't use any Trainer cards from their hand.

Name: Iron Valiant
Ability-Effect: Attacks used by your Future Pokémon cost 1 less [C] Energy.
Flavor-Text: It’s possible that this is the object listed as Iron Valiant in a certain expedition journal.

Name: Iron Leaves
Effects: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: According to the few eyewitness accounts that exist, it used its shining blades to julienne large trees and boulders.

Name: Iron Boulder
Effects: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It was named after a mysterious object recorded in an old book. Its body seems to be metallic.

Name: Iron Crown
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: There was supposedly an incident in which it launched shining blades to cut everything around it to pieces. Little else is known about it.

Name: Nacli
Flavor-Text: The ground scrapes its body as it travels, causing it to leave salt behind. Salt is constantly being created and replenished inside Nacli’s body.

Name: Naclstack
Flavor-Text: It compresses rock salt inside its body and shoots out hardened salt pellets with enough force to perforate an iron sheet.

Name: Garganacl
Ability-Effect: During Pokémon Checkup, heal 10 damage from each of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Many Pokémon gather around Garganacl, hoping to lick at its mineral-rich salt.

Name: Great Tusk
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This creature resembles a mysterious Pokémon that, according to a paranormal magazine, has lived since ancient times.

Name: Sandy Shocks
Effects: Switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot. If you do, this attack does 50 damage to the new Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It slightly resembles a Magneton that lived for 10,000 years and was featured in an article in a paranormal magazine.

Name: Koraidon ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you put this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench, you may switch it with your Active Pokémon. If you do, move all of your Energy in play to this Pokémon.
Effects: Discard the top card of your deck.

Name: Umbreon
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: When darkness falls, the rings on the body begin to glow, striking fear in the hearts of anyone nearby.

Name: Sneasel
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This is a smart and sneaky Pokémon. A pair may work together to steal eggs by having one lure the parents away.

Name: Weavile
Effects: If this Pokémon evolved from Sneasel during this turn, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: They travel in groups of four or five, leaving signs for one another on trees and rocks. They bring down their prey with coordinated attacks.

Name: Sableye
Effects: Take a random Energy from among [G], [R], [W], [L], [P], [F], [D], and [M] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Materials from gems it has eaten float to the surface of its body and can form an infinite number of patterns among individuals.

Name: Pawniard
Flavor-Text: A pack of these Pokémon forms to serve a Bisharp boss. Each Pawniard trains diligently, dreaming of one day taking the lead.

Name: Bisharp
Flavor-Text: It’s accompanied by a large retinue of Pawniard. Bisharp keeps a keen eye on its minions, ensuring none of them even think of double-crossing it.

Name: Kingambit
Effects: This attack does 40 more damage for each time your Pokémon have been Knocked Out during this game.
Flavor-Text: Only a Bisharp that stands above all others in its vast army can evolve into Kingambit.

Name: Glimmet
Flavor-Text: Glimmet’s toxic mineral crystals look just like flower petals. This Pokémon scatters poisonous powder like pollen to protect itself.

Name: Glimmora
Ability-Effect: When this Pokémon is Knocked Out, flip a coin. If heads, your opponent can't get any points for it.
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Glimmora’s petals are made of crystallized poison energy. It has recently become evident that these petals resemble Tera Jewels.

Name: Iron Jugulis
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, do 20 damage to the Attacking Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It’s possible that Iron Jugulis, an object described in an old book, may actually be this Pokémon.

Name: Roaring Moon
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you put this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench, you may switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: According to an article in a dubious magazine, this Pokémon has some connection to a phenomenon that occurs in a certain region.

Name: Corviknight
Flavor-Text: Although its wings have partly turned to steel and become heavy as a result, this Pokémon flies through the skies with ease.

Name: Cufant
Flavor-Text: Cufant can lift loads weighing five tons. In the mornings, it heads into caves with its herd, in search of the ore on which these Pokémon feed.

Name: Copperajah
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 100 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Copperajah are prideful, cantankerous Pokémon. Specimens with vibrant green skin command the respect of others of their kind.

Name: Iron Treads
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Sightings of this Pokémon have occurred in recent years. It resembles a mysterious object described in an old expedition journal.

Name: Altaria
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: On sunny days, it flies freely through the sky and blends into the clouds. It sings in a beautiful soprano.

Name: Walking Wake
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon, and this attack also does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It resembles an illustration published in a paranormal magazine, said to be a depiction of a super-ancient Suicune.

Name: Gouging Fire
Effects: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −30 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text: It resembles an eerie Pokémon once shown in a paranormal magazine. That Pokémon was said to be an Entei regenerated from a fossil.

Name: Raging Bolt
Effects: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon. Knock Out your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It bears resemblance to a Pokémon that became a hot topic for a short while after a paranormal magazine touted it as Raikou’s ancestor.

Name: Eevee
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.
Flavor-Text: Its genetic code is irregular. It may mutate if it is exposed to radiation from element stones.

Name: Girafarig
Flavor-Text: Girafarig’s tail has a small head. It instinctively bites at any foe that approaches the Pokémon from behind.

Name: Farigiraf
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: The hardened head from the tail protects the head of the main body as Farigiraf whips its long neck around to headbutt enemies.

Name: Dunsparce
Flavor-Text: If spotted, it escapes by burrowing with its tail. It can float just slightly using its wings.

Name: Dudunsparce
Effects: If this Pokémon evolved from Dunsparce during this turn, discard 2 random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It drives enemies out of its nest by sucking in enough air to fill its long, narrow lungs, then releasing the air in an intense blast.

Name: Swablu
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It can’t relax if it or its surroundings are not clean. It wipes off dirt with its wings.

Name: Rufflet
Flavor-Text: With its sharp claws, this Pokémon pierces its prey, and then it pecks at them. Although it also consumes berries, it’s a carnivore at heart.

Name: Braviary
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: For the sake of its friends, this brave warrior of the sky will not stop battling, even if injured.

Name: Hawlucha
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Hawlucha live quietly without flocking together. They fight constantly with their natural enemies: noisy groups of Primeape.

Name: Rookidee
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: The females are fussier than the males. If another creature dirties a female Rookidee’s wings, it’ll peck the offender relentlessly in a burning rage.

Name: Corvisquire
Flavor-Text: This intelligent Pokémon will quickly learn how to use any tool it can hold in its beak or its talons.

Name: Flamigo
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Thanks to a behavior of theirs known as “synchronizing,” an entire flock of these Pokémon can attack simultaneously in perfect harmony.

Name: Terapagos ex
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each type of Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Ancient Booster Energy Capsule
Description: The Ancient Pokémon this card is attached to gets +40 HP.

Name: Future Booster Energy Capsule
Description: Attacks used by the Future Pokémon this card is attached to do +20 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

Name: Juliana
Description: Put a random Stage 2 Pokémon from your deck into your hand.

Name: Professor Sada
Description: Attach 3 different types of Energy from your discard pile to your Ancient Pokémon in any way you like.

Name: Professor Turo
Description: Shuffle 1 of your Future Pokémon in play into your deck.

Name: Area Zero
Description: Once during each player's turn, that player may shuffle a Basic Pokémon from their hand into their deck. If they do, they draw a card.

Flavor-Text: Within its internal organs, Snom amplifies the frigid air it gets from eating snow and then uses this amplified air to create icicle-like spikes.

Effects: If this is the first time this Pokémon has used an attack after coming into play, during your opponent's next turn, they can't use any Trainer cards from their hand.

Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you put this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench, you may switch it with your Active Pokémon. If you do, move all of your Energy in play to this Pokémon.
Effects: Discard the top card of your deck.

Name: Terapagos ex
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each type of Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Rellor
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It rolls its mud ball around while the energy it needs for evolution matures. Eventually the time comes for it to evolve.

Name: Charcadet
Flavor-Text: Its firepower increases when it fights, reaching over 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit. It likes berries that are rich in fat.

Name: Tadbulb
Effects: Take a [L] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It floats using the electricity stored in its body. When thunderclouds are around, Tadbulb will float higher off the ground.

Name: Rabsca
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: An infant sleeps inside the ball. Rabsca rolls the ball soothingly with its legs to ensure the infant sleeps comfortably.

Name: Diglett
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Its skin is very thin. If it is exposed to light, its blood heats up, causing it to grow weak.

Name: Dugtrio
Flavor-Text: In battle, it digs through the ground and strikes the unsuspecting foe from an unexpected direction.

Name: Klawf
Effects: This Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon lives on sheer cliffs. It sidesteps opponents’ attacks, then lunges for their weak spots with its claws.

Name: Orthworm
Effects: Take a [M] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon lives in arid deserts. It maintains its metal body by consuming iron from the soil.

Name: Swablu
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It can’t relax if it or its surroundings are not clean. It wipes off dirt with its wings.

Name: Altaria
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: On sunny days, it flies freely through the sky and blends into the clouds. It sings in a beautiful soprano.

Name: Meowscarada ex
Effects: Choose a spot from among your opponent's Active Spot and Bench. At the end of your opponent's next turn, do 70 damage to the Pokémon in the spot you chose.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Armarouge ex
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes −30 damage from attacks.
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy from this Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Bellibolt ex
Effects: If you have 4 or more [L] Energy in play, this attack does 70 more damage.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Mega Altaria ex
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Iron Valiant
Ability-Effect: Attacks used by your Future Pokémon cost 1 less [C] Energy.
Flavor-Text: It’s possible that this is the object listed as Iron Valiant in a certain expedition journal.

Flavor-Text: According to an article in a dubious magazine, this Pokémon has some connection to a phenomenon that occurs in a certain region.
